title: Work Ticket: Rate Limiter API
description: Day-to-day engineering ticket: add a rate-limited endpoint to a small service. AI tools allowed. Submit a PR.
pmBrief: Platform needs per-IP rate limiting on the public API before we open beta traffic. Ship a reviewed PR, not a whiteboard puzzle.
ticketBrief:
## Ticket PLAT-1842

In the starter repo, add token-bucket rate limiting for `POST /api/echo`:

- 10 requests per minute per client IP
- Return `429` with `Retry-After` header when limited
- Unit tests for limiter logic
- Update README with run + test instructions

**AI assistants allowed** — note what you used in the PR description.

**Submit:** open a PR against `main`. Codespaces, local, or devcontainer (Java/Kotlin variants use the same ticket via language-specific starter branches).
constraints: AI assistants allowed — disclose in PR description
No secrets or API keys in repo

title: Work Ticket: Point-in-Time Backtest Linter
description: Quant research engineering ticket: build a linter that flags look-ahead bias patterns in a toy feature pipeline. SIG / prop-shop style — tests matter more than slides.
pmBrief: Research keeps shipping features with accidental future data. We need an automated linter on the standard pipeline template before any backtest runs.
ticketBrief:
## Ticket QUANT-771

In the starter repo (Python), implement `pit_linter.py`:

- Load sample feature parquet + price series with intentional leakage examples
- Flag rows where feature timestamp > decision timestamp (as-of violation)
- Flag merge keys that don't respect point-in-time joins
- Emit JSON report: `{violations: [...], rows_checked, pass}`
- Unit tests covering at least 3 violation types
- README: how to run linter in CI before backtest

**AI allowed** — disclose in PR.

**Submit:** PR against `main` with tests green.
constraints: No live market data APIs required — use bundled samples
exampleDeliverables: Sample linter JSON output in PR

title: Work Ticket: VWAP Execution Simulator
description: Implement a minimal VWAP execution simulator: given intraday volume curve + order size, output expected slippage vs naive market order.
pmBrief: Execution desk wants a quick what-if tool before committing size. MVP CLI or notebook is fine — correctness and tests over UI polish.
ticketBrief:
## Ticket EXEC-204

- Input: minute bars (volume, vwap, close) + parent order size
- Output: simulated fill path, avg price, slippage vs arrival
- Support linear participation rate cap (e.g. max 5% of bar volume)
- Tests: deterministic fixture, edge case empty volume bar
- README with example run

**Submit:** PR with tests.
exampleDeliverables: Example slippage report in PR description

title: Work Ticket: Order Book Snapshot Stats
description: Parse L2 snapshot CSVs and compute spread, mid, imbalance, and microprice — typical quant dev screen follow-up.
pmBrief: Market data team needs a standard stats bundle on every snapshot file before it enters research sandboxes.
ticketBrief:
## Ticket MD-88

- Parse bundled CSV snapshots (bid/ask price/size columns)
- Compute per row: spread, mid, volume imbalance, microprice
- Flag crossed books (bid > ask) into a reject file
- Summary stats JSON: median spread, % crossed, sample count
- pytest on bundled fixtures

**Submit:** PR against main.
constraints: Must not silently drop >1% rows without logging

title: Pairs Spread Monitor
description: Build a pairs trading monitor: ingest two price series, compute spread/z-score, alert when |z|>2. Deployable dashboard or CLI with chart export.
pmBrief: Research wants a dead-simple monitor for a candidate pair before we commit capital. Historical CSV in, signals out.
constraints: Use bundled sample CSVs — no paid data feeds
Must run locally in <2 min setup
exampleDeliverables: GitHub repo URL or live demo

title: Habit Tracker
description: Ship a minimal habit tracker with daily check-ins and streaks.
pmBrief: Users want a dead-simple habit tracker. MVP: add habit, mark done today, see streak. Mobile-friendly.
constraints: Must be deployable via public URL

title: AI Chat Widget
description: Single-page chat UI with system prompt, message history, and basic guardrails.
pmBrief: Embed a support-style chat widget. Must show user/assistant roles and block empty sends.

title: Expense Splitter
description: Split bills among friends with shares and settle-up summary.
pmBrief: Roommates need to log expenses and see who owes whom.

title: Document Q&A Bot
description: RAG chatbot over uploaded markdown or PDF snippets with citations.
pmBrief: Query internal docs with cited answers. Handle empty retrieval.

title: Lightweight CRM
description: CRM with contacts, pipeline stages, and notes.
pmBrief: Startup needs basic lead tracking without Salesforce complexity.

title: Eval Dashboard
description: Upload prompts and model outputs; score with rubric and compare runs.
pmBrief: AI team needs to regression-test prompt changes visually.
constraints: Store runs locally or simple DB

title: Micro-SaaS MVP
description: Niche productivity tool with auth and Stripe test payments.
pmBrief: Validate one paid workflow. Stripe test mode acceptable.

title: Agent Workflow Tool
description: Multi-step agent that calls tools (search, calc) with visible trace.
pmBrief: Demonstrate planner + tool execution with logging for debugging.
